channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge says Justice Department attacked her character to 'impugn the integrity' of US judicial system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge accused the Justice Department on Wednesday of attacking her character in an effort to undermine the integrity of the judicial system, forcefully pushing back against the Trump administration's criticism of the courts for rulings that blocked parts of the president'
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Atlantic releases the Signal chat showing Hegseth's detailed attack plans against the Houthis
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Atlantic on Wednesday released the entire Signal chat among senior national security officials, showing that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth provided the exact timings of warplane launches and when bombs would drop — before the men and women flying those attacks against Yemen’s
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Hoax Signal
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Facing possible primary challenge from Trump ally, long-serving senator gets backing of leader Thune
item.description: Republican Sen. John Cornyn of Texas, facing a possible 2026 primary challenge from Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, a President Trump ally, lands the endorsement of Senate Majority Leader John Thune.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US could run short of money to pay its bills by August without a debt limit deal, CBO says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is on track to hit its statutory debt ceiling — the so-called X-date when the country runs short of money to pay its bills— as early as August without a deal between lawmakers and the White House, according to a Congressional Budget Office report Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump will announce auto tariffs at a White House news conference
item.description: President Donald Trump will announce tariffs on auto imports, a move the White Houses claims would foster domestic manufacturing that could also put a financial squeeze on automakers that depend on global supply chains.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Mike Johnson and Jim Jordan could hit back at judges blocking Trump's agenda
item.description: House Republicans are looking at multiple lanes to take on activist judges blocking President Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gabbard calls Signal chats a 'mistake' as Trump officials face grilling over leaked military plan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard said Wednesday it was a “mistake” for national security officials to discuss sensitive military plans on a group text chain that also included a journalist — a leak that has roiled President Donald Trump's national security leadership
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Republicans look to DOGE model for state government efficiency commission
item.description: The commission would consist of a panel of six citizens who would investigate fraud in state agencies and potential conflicts of interest in the Legislature. Three members would be appointed by Republicans and three by Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Kristi Noem says US has almost total control of border
item.description: High-level cartel associates and gang members are still trying to slip through — but law enforcement is intercepting them.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: NPR, PBS defend their 'essential' need during heated DOGE hearing
item.description: At stake were potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Academic groups sue over deportations of international students, scholars
item.description: The federal government revoked the student visa of one Columbia student and is looking to take away the green card of another.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Why Israel's having some of its biggest protests since the war in Gaza began
item.description: They're demanding a deal between Israel and Hamas to release all the remaining hostages, and also demonstrating against government attempts to weaken the judiciary.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf and Daniel Estrin, MPR News

item.title: Trump officials texted war plans to a group chat in a secure app that included a journalist
item.description: Top national security officials for President Donald Trump, including his defense secretary, texted war plans for upcoming military strikes in Yemen to a group chat in a secure messaging app that included the editor-in-chief for The Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's plan to dismantle Education Dept. criticized by Nevada Dems
item.description: Nevada Democrats are criticizing President Donald Trump's effort to dismantle the US Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House: 'Critical functions' will stay under Department of Education
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said all the critical functions of the Department of Education will remain ahead of President Trump signing an executive order to dismantle the federal agency.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
